title: matelea costaricensis
intro: Matelea costaricensis, also known as the climbing milkvine or dog's tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Central America, particularly in Costa Rica, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues. Matelea costaricensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions.
